channel.title: ExxonMobil on The Guyana Brief
channel.description: Recent content in ExxonMobil on The Guyana Brief

item.title: Patriots Portfolio — March 27, 2026: Streets, Ships, and Sovereignty
item.description: This week: Exxon starts building Oil Ship No. 8 without government approval, the government takes 22 Georgetown streets, and Guyana keeps printing economic records. The Patriots Portfolio tracks the business of Guyana.

item.title: Wednesday Brief: Oil Hits $100, Mohameds Rejected Again, and Guyana's Last Independent Paper is Gone
item.description: Oil hits triple digits, the Court of Appeal delivers another blow to the Mohameds, Guyana's cash grant starts flowing to people who already have bank accounts, and we say goodbye to Stabroek News.

item.title: De Boys Seh: Exxon Collecting Ships Like Sticker Book
item.description: De boys at the rum shop react to Exxon owning all four FPSOs, Dr. Singh's Pablo Escobar moment, and the budget debate that was better than any movie.

item.title: Sunday Brief: Exxon Now Owns ALL Four Oil Ships, Finance Minister Drops Pablo Escobar Reference, and a Miracle Baby at GPHC
item.description: ExxonMobil completes its FPSO collection like Pokémon cards, Dr. Singh invokes Pablo Escobar in Parliament, GPHC doctors save mother and baby from a rare bleeding disorder, and the Opposition Leader says the budget won't lift anyone out of poverty. Happy Sunday, Guyana.

item.title: Uncle Ramesh Take: Dem Cyant See Progress Even When It Hit Dem in de Face
item.description: Uncle Ramesh reads the Sunday papers and explains why the opposition can't recognise progress even when it building a school next to them.
